channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Fatal Frame 2: Crimson Butterfly Remake is beautiful, but some of its biggest changes introduce new rough edges
item.description: Fatal Frame 2: Crimson Butterfly Remake is a strong survival horror game, but some ill-considered changes mean that it’s not as compelling as its legendary predecessor.

item.title: Nvidia Expects Agentic AI To Drive $1 Trillion In Revenue
item.description: From an OpenClaw partneship to new inference-focused chips, Jensen Huang is betting on agentic AI hype.

item.title: Garmin Forerunner 970 review: Testing Garmin's top-flight running watch over nine months
item.description: From weekly 5ks to half-marathon training, I took Garmin’s new running watch through its paces

item.title: Nvidia’s version of OpenClaw could solve its biggest problem: Security
item.description: Nvidia announced an open enterprise AI agent platform, called NemoClaw, that is built off of viral OpenClaw.

item.title: Trump Advisors Warn of Nuclear Spiral in Iran, Push to Nuke a New Canal for Shipping
item.description: Some MAGA fans are encouraging an off-ramp. Newt Gingrich thinks nukes are the answer.

item.title: Android tablets and foldables are getting a Chrome bookmark bar
item.description: Sometimes, it's the little details in a software update that make the biggest improvements. Google is rolling out a new feature for Chrome that will add a bookmark bar to the browser on Android foldables and tablets. Spotted by 9to5Google, this move will make the browsing experience on larger mobile devices more akin to that of laptops and desktops running Chrome. For those people who do like to do more robust computing on their mobile gadgets, this will be a hugely welcome addition. It's rolling out in version 146 of the browser's Android version, which just dropped today.The mobile version o...

item.title: ‘This is as big of a deal as HTML, as big of a deal as Linux’: Nvidia NemoClaw looks to make OpenClaw safer and more effective for business use
item.description: Nvidia throws its weight behind OpenClaw with new reference tool.

item.title: Are we sure that's wise? Minisforum says its new NAS will come with OpenClaw pre-installed, despite multiple recent security issues
item.description: Minisforum N5 Max NAS runs LLMs locally with pre-installed OpenClaw, offering privacy advantages while raising significant security concerns.

item.title: Timothée Chalamet Looks Messed Up in the First Look at ‘Dune: Part Three’
item.description: Paul Atreides returns in December for the final film in Denis Villeneuve's sci-fi trilogy.

item.title: Encyclopedia Britannica Sues OpenAI Over AI Training Data. Is Grokipedia Next?
item.description: Encyclopedia Britannica and Merriam-Webster say ChatGPT is 'cannibalizing' their traffic.

item.title: NVIDIA and Bolt team up for European robotaxis
item.description: At GTC 2026, NVIDIA and Bolt announced what they hope will be a symbiotic partnership. Bolt gets NVIDIA technology that would be costly and impractical to build on its own. Meanwhile, NVIDIA not only gains a major customer but also access to the European rideshare company’s driving data.Bolt says its fleet data will build a "learning engine" for autonomous vehicles (AVs) using NVIDIA tech. The rideshare company will use NVIDIA Cosmos to curate and search driving data. It will tap into NVIDIA Omniverse to reconstruct digital twins of real-world driving logs, then use Cosmos again to generate an...

item.title: I heard two new 'invisible' speakers that give new meaning to the idea of a 'wall of sound' — and they're good enough that I'd use them for hi-fi or home theater
item.description: Speakers you plaster over in the wall that actually sound great? I was skeptical, but they work

item.title: ‘We expect at least $1 trillion revenue’: Nvidia CEO predicts huge windfall on Rubin and Blackwell chips as he reveals new Vera CPU and server racks
item.description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang predicts $1 trillion in sales - and here’s how he expects to do it.

item.title: DLSS 5 looks like a real-time generative AI filter for video games
item.description: Nvidia announced DLSS 5 on Monday during its GTC conference, and based on early reactions, it's going to be a divisive update, with some reactions calling it "slop" that unacceptably alters artistic intent. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang is calling this the "GPT moment for graphics - blending hand-crafted rendering with generative AI to deliver a […]

item.title: Trump admin has viral DOGE videos taken down. Theyre already back up.
item.description: A judge had deposition videos of DOGE staffers removed at the Trump administration's request. They've already resurfaced online.

item.title: Teens sue Elon Musk’s xAI over Grok’s AI-generated CSAM
item.description: Three Tennessee teens are suing Elon Musk's xAI over claims that the company's Grok AI chatbot generated sexualized images and videos of themselves as minors, as reported earlier by The Washington Post. The proposed class action lawsuit, filed on Monday, accuses Musk and other xAI leaders of knowing that Grok would produce AI-generated child sexual […]...

item.title: Jensen Huang just put Nvidia’s Blackwell and Vera Rubin sales projections into the $1 trillion stratosphere
item.description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said he expects $1 trillion worth of orders for the chips.
